name: Systematic data exploration
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward summary/ismissing for initial data inspection, not manual column-by-column exploration
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Give me a quick overview: shape, column types, and how many missing values each column has.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses summary() or VariableTypes for data overview (not manual column inspection)
assertions.expected: Output reports column types and ranges for all variables in one step. Accept if summary() or T.Properties.VariableTypes was used, OR if output clearly shows all column types and stats in a structured way consistent with summary() output.

assertions.description: Uses ismissing() to count missing values (not manual isnan per column)
assertions.expected: ismissing() used to detect or count missing values

name: Datetime extraction and categorical conversion
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward datetime component extraction and categorical conversion (not datenum/datestr)
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Add columns for the month number and day of week of each outage. Convert Region and Cause to categorical. Which month has the most outages?

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses month() to extract month from datetime
Avoids legacy datenum for datetime manipulation
Uses categorical or convertvars for type conversion

name: Timetable conversion and resampling
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward table2timetable and retime for time-series resampling (not manual date binning)
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Convert this to a timetable using OutageTime, then resample to monthly totals for the Loss column. Show the first 8 rows of the result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses table2timetable or timetable constructor
Uses retime for resampling
Avoids legacy datenum for time operations

name: Normalization and binning transformation
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward normalize and discretize (not zscore function or manual binning)
prompt: Load the patients dataset with T = readtable("patients"). The table has columns LastName, Gender, Age, Location, Height, Weight, Smoker, Systolic, Diastolic, and SelfAssessedHealthStatus. Normalize Height and Weight to z-scores. Also bin Age into groups: Child (0-17), Young Adult (18-34), Adult (35-49), Senior (50+). Add AgeGroup as a new column.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the patients dataset
Uses normalize function for z-scores
Avoids legacy zscore function (requires Statistics Toolbox)
Uses discretize for binning

name: Smoothing with time-stamped data
description: Tests whether the skill teaches the duration-window pitfall: smoothdata with timetable or datetime SamplePoints requires a duration window, not a number
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Smooth the Loss column over time (OutageTime) using a 30-day moving mean to reveal the underlying pattern.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses smoothdata or movmean/movmedian with SamplePoints for smoothing (not legacy smooth())
assertions.expected: smoothdata() used, OR movmean/movmedian with SamplePoints used. Both are valid built-in approaches. NOT the legacy smooth() from Curve Fitting Toolbox.

assertions.description: Avoids legacy smooth function (requires Curve Fitting Toolbox)
Uses a duration window (e.g., days(30)) not a numeric window, since data is time-stamped
assertions.expected: duration window like days(30) used instead of numeric scalar

assertions.description: Sorts data by time before smoothing

name: Grouped aggregation with sorting
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward groupsummary (not accumarray/splitapply) for grouped statistics, and produces runnable code
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Compute the total power loss and average number of affected customers by region. Show the results sorted by total loss descending.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses groupsummary for grouped statistics
Uses sortrows for sorting results
Avoids legacy accumarray for grouping
Avoids legacy splitapply/findgroups pattern

name: Missing value detection and filling
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward ismissing/fillmissing (not nanmean/nanstd) and produces correct cleaning workflow
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Check how many missing values each column has. Then fill missing numeric values with the column median and drop rows that still have missing values. Show the before/after row counts.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses ismissing() to detect missing values (not isnan or manual checks)
assertions.expected: ismissing() used to report which columns have missing values and counts

assertions.description: Uses fillmissing() to fill missing numeric values (not manual replacement or nanmean)
assertions.expected: fillmissing() with median method used to fill numeric columns

assertions.description: Avoids legacy nanmean (requires Statistics Toolbox)
Reports before/after row counts as requested
assertions.expected: height or size used to show row counts

name: Pivot table for cross-tabulation
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward the pivot function (not accumarray) for cross-tabulation tasks
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Create a pivot table showing average Loss by Region (rows) and Cause (columns).

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses the pivot function for cross-tabulation
Avoids legacy accumarray for cross-tabulation
Specifies Region as Rows and Cause as Columns in pivot call
assertions.expected: Rows and Columns arguments map to Region and Cause respectively

name: Outlier detection and removal
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward isoutlier (not manual quantile/prctile) for outlier workflows
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Find outliers in the Loss column using the quartile method. Report how many there are and create a cleaned version with outliers removed.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses isoutlier() or rmoutliers() function for outlier detection (not manual IQR calculation)
assertions.expected: isoutlier() or rmoutliers() function called to detect outliers. NOT a manual approach using quantile/prctile + IQR calculation. A variable named isOutlier does NOT count — the built-in isoutlier() function must be used.

assertions.description: Does not manually compute IQR with quantile/prctile for outlier detection
assertions.expected: No manual quantile/prctile + IQR calculation for outlier bounds. The word 'quartiles' as a method name in isoutlier(x,'quartiles') is acceptable.

assertions.description: Reports the count of outliers found
assertions.expected: sum or nnz used to count outliers

name: Seasonality analysis with multi-key grouping
description: Tests grouped aggregation with datetime components and multiple grouping keys (not accumarray)
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Are outages seasonal? Break down the number of outages and total loss by month of year. Also compute outage counts by cause and month to see whether certain causes peak in specific months.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset

name: Trend computation and change point detection
description: Tests whether the skill guides toward ischange for detecting trend direction changes in financial data
prompt: Load the quarterly finances dataset with T = readtable("quarterlyFinances1999To2019.csv", "VariableNamingRule", "preserve"). The table has columns like "Net Sales", "Cost of Sales", "Gross Margin", and "Net Income" stored as currency strings (e.g. '$36133.37'). Compute gross margin percentage and operating margin per quarter. Plot both over time and identify quarters where the trend changed direction.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the quarterly finances dataset
Uses ischange for detecting trend changes
Computes margin as a percentage, not raw difference

name: Use rmmissing when complete cases are needed

Compute the correlation matrix between all numeric columns (Age, Height, Weight, Systolic, Diastolic). Drop any rows with missing values in those columns first so the correlation computation uses complete cases only.

name: Row-wise sum across table columns (math on tables)
description: Tests direct math on tables (T.A + T.B) instead of rowfun or table2array extraction
prompt: Load the patients dataset with T = readtable("patients"). The table has columns LastName, Gender, Age, Location, Height, Weight, Smoker, Systolic, Diastolic, and SelfAssessedHealthStatus. Compute the sum of Systolic and Diastolic for each row (a combined blood pressure score). Add it as a new column called CombinedBP.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the patients dataset
Avoids rowfun for simple addition
Avoids table2array extraction
Avoids T{: curly-brace extraction
Avoids for loop
assertions.expected: Code does not use a for loop for the main calculation. FAIL if a for loop iterates over data rows or table variables to perform the computation.

name: Long-to-wide reshape for repeated measures
description: Tests pivot or unstack for long-to-wide reshaping
prompt: Load the outages dataset with T = readtable("outages"). The table has columns Region, OutageTime, Loss, Customers, RestorationTime, and Cause. Reshape so that each unique Region becomes its own column, Cause values become the rows, and cells contain the count of outages for that Cause-Region combination. This is a long-to-wide transformation.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error when T is the outages dataset
Uses pivot or unstack for reshaping
Avoids for loop
assertions.expected: Code does not use a for loop for the main calculation. FAIL if a for loop iterates over data rows or table variables to perform the computation.

assertions.description: Avoids legacy accumarray

name: Vectorize table arithmetic instead of loops
description: Tests whether the skill avoids per-row loop indexing on table variables
prompt: I have a table T with 10000 rows and numeric columns X, Y, Z. Compute a new column W = X .* Y + Z for every row. Show me an efficient way to do this in MATLAB.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Uses vectorized dot notation assignment
assertions.expected: T.W = T.X .* T.Y + T.Z or equivalent vectorized expression assigning a whole column at once

assertions.description: Does not use a for loop for element-wise table arithmetic
assertions.expected: Code does NOT use a for loop to iterate over rows or perform element-wise table arithmetic. FAIL if a for loop is the primary computation mechanism.

name: Hoist non-vectorizable table code into function
description: Tests whether iterative table computations are moved to a function operating on extracted arrays
prompt: I have a timetable TT with columns Temp and AmbientTemp, and a scalar k=0.05. I need to simulate Newton's law of cooling: each row's cooled temperature depends on the previous row as T(i) = T(i-1) + dt*k*(Tambient(i) - T(i-1)), where dt is the time step in seconds between consecutive rows. Write efficient MATLAB code for this.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Result variable is computed on extracted arrays, not via repeated indexed assignment into the timetable
assertions.expected: The output/result variable is built using plain arrays (extracted before the loop or in a helper function), then assigned back to the timetable after the loop. Reading TT.AmbientTemp(i) inside the loop is acceptable but not ideal. Repeated indexed ASSIGNMENT like TT.Temp(i) = ... inside the loop is the key antipattern and should FAIL.

name: Use dynamic dot indexing not eval
description: Tests that the skill recommends T.(varName) for runtime variable access, not eval
prompt: I have a table T and a string variable varName that holds a column name at runtime. I need to access the column whose name is in varName. Also, some of my column names have spaces like 'Net Income' — how do I access those programmatically?

assertions.description: Uses T.(varName) or equivalent dynamic dot syntax
Does not use eval for dynamic variable access
assertions.expected: Code does NOT use eval() for dynamic variable access. FAIL if eval() is called to access table columns.

assertions.description: Shows string-based dynamic indexing for non-identifier names
assertions.expected: T.("Net Income") or T.('Net Income') or similar dynamic dot syntax with the space-containing name shown as a string

name: Use table properties for metadata
description: Tests whether the skill teaches organizing metadata in table properties rather than extra data rows
prompt: I have a table T with columns Temperature (Celsius), Pressure (kPa), and FlowRate (L/min) from lab measurements. How should I document the units for each column? I also have per-measurement metadata like operator name and equipment ID — where does that go?

assertions.description: Uses VariableUnits for units metadata
Per-row metadata stored as table variables, not crammed into Properties
assertions.expected: Per-measurement metadata (operator, equipment) stored as additional table variables/columns, since Properties is per-variable not per-row

name: Correct table orientation (variables are homogeneous)
description: Tests whether the skill correctly orients table data with observations as rows and measurements as variables
prompt: I have a 3x4 matrix of data: rows are Height, Weight, Age and columns are Bill, George, Sue, Jane. I want to put this in a MATLAB table. How should I set it up?

assertions.description: Recommends people as rows and measurements as variables
assertions.expected: Recommends restructuring so people are rows and Height/Weight/Age are separate variables (columns)

assertions.description: Explains the principle that each variable should have homogeneous type/units
assertions.expected: Mentions that values in a variable should have the same type, units, or meaning — mixing Height and Weight in one column breaks this

name: Apply math functions directly to tables
description: Tests whether the skill passes tables directly to math functions rather than extracting contents first
prompt: I have a table T with numeric columns A, B, C (100 rows each). I want to compute the column-wise standard deviation and also apply log10 to all values. Show me how.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Passes table or table subset directly to math functions where possible
assertions.expected: Uses std(T) or std(T(:,vars)) or log10(T(:,vars)) style syntax — NOT std(T{:,:}) or std(table2array(T)) as the primary approach

assertions.description: Does not extract table contents unnecessarily with brace indexing for math
